facts.context: Heat is applied to a beaker of water, raising the water temperature a few degrees. What happens to the water molecules after heat is applied?
facts.truth: The molecules spread apart and move more quickly.
distractors: The molecules spread apart and move more slowly.
The molecules come together and move more slowly.
The molecules come together and move more quickly.

facts.context: A positively-charged hydrogen ion has a much smaller radius than a neutral hydrogen atom. Which best explains this difference?
facts.truth: Electrons are located far from the nuclei of their atoms.
distractors: The nucleus is the least dense region of an atom.
Most of the volume of atoms is filled with the particles they contain.
The repulsion of protons and neutrons keeps them on opposite ends of atoms.

facts.context: Sodium chloride is formed when sodium and chlorine chemically combine. Which term best describes sodium chloride?
Which subatomic particle has a negative charge?
One difference between a chemical formula and a chemical equation is that the chemical equation shows
facts.truth: the chemical reaction that takes place.
distractors: the number of atoms per element.
what elements are in each molecule.

facts.context: Which chemical is a compound?
The electrical charge of the nucleus of an atom is +12. How many electrons does the atom have in its neutral state?
Which of the following results when hydrogen and chlorine chemically bond to form hydrogen chloride?
Which part of digestion is mainly a chemical change?
facts.truth: Stomach acids dissolve food into nutrients.
distractors: Teeth break food into small pieces.
Throat muscles push food toward the stomach.
Intestinal cells transport nutrients into the blood.

facts.context: Two atoms of oxygen combine to form pure oxygen gas. What is the electrical charge of the O_{2} molecules?
What happens to the molecules of a liquid when the liquid cools?
The conservation of matter can be demonstrated by using chemical equations. Which chemical equation demonstrates the conservation of matter?
Which statement best describes the molecules in a sample of water that was heated from a liquid to a gas?
facts.truth: The average kinetic energy of the molecules increased.
distractors: The average size of the molecules decreased.
The average size of the molecules increased.
The average kinetic energy of the molecules decreased.

facts.context: Which type of substances serves as a catalyst for reactions that break large food molecules into smaller useful molecules?
Which is the best example of a chemical reaction?
facts.truth: oxygen absorbs sunlight forming ozone molecules
distractors: sunlight evaporates water from a lake
a prism refracts sunlight into light of different wavelengths

facts.context: Which set of elements has similar bonding properties?
When a neutral atom gains or loses electrons, it becomes
All of these elements are metals except
What are the parts that make up an atom?
What do the elements sulfur (S), nitrogen (N), phosphorus (P), and bromine (Br) have in common?
distractors: They have the same thermal conductivity.
They have the same number of protons.
